models.model_label: mCODE ontological conceptual model (OntoUML) grounding the EUCAIM hyper-ontology

evidence_quotes.quote: The operational version of the mCODE ontological model is generated as an OWL file [13].
The semantic integration experiment with the bottom-up version of the hyper-ontology [13] is conducted as follows.
The grounding process considers developing the mCODE core ontological model that reflects the main ontological semantics of the oncology domain.

goals.justification: The model is actively used to generate an operational OWL artifact and to integrate/build the hyper-ontology's core and upper layers, matching the Intervening signal of 'generated artifact' and 'used to enable interoperability'.

evidence_quotes.quote: Facing this heterogeneity and to clarify these concepts' specifications and medical aspects, we refer to the minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCODE) 3 [1] largely adopted within healthcare establishments which are increasingly implementing FHIR 4 .
The intended model aims to capture and explicitly and unambiguously represent the basic concepts of the oncology domain and their connections.

The ontological distinctions of UFO have permitted, using OntoUML, to uncover and clarify the nature of real-world entities from the oncology domain (e.g., morphology/disease) and explicitly represent the ontological nature of these entities.

goals.justification: The model is explicitly framed as a means to clarify and unambiguously capture oncology concepts and their nature, directly matching the Understanding signals 'clarify', 'conceptualize', and building domain knowledge.

evidence_quotes.quote: The top-down approach has helped overcome conflicting or heterogeneous descriptions of oncology concepts, maintaining coherent ontological content and semantic interoperability.

uncertain_goals.reason: The text describes identifying and overcoming heterogeneity, but the same evidence is tightly tied to building the hyper-ontology artifact (Intervening); per the disambiguation rule, when the same evidence shows implementation it should be coded only as Intervening unless separate evidence supports decision-making.
